name: Read current state
check: Inspect the relevant files, services, and registry before editing.
why: Prevents stale assumptions and protects user changes.

name: Exclude backups by default
check: Do not broad-search backups unless the task is specifically about backup content.
why: Archived logs may contain secrets and high-noise generated state.

name: Trust structured registries
check: Prefer config/*.tsv and dashboard data before prose files.
why: Small records are faster for LLMs and easier to diff.

name: Use apply_patch for manual edits
check: Manual source edits must go through apply_patch.
why: Preserves intentional diffs and avoids accidental file rewrites.

name: Respect dirty worktrees
check: Check git status and never revert unrelated user edits.
why: The machine is actively evolving and may contain user work.

name: Update source plus UI
check: When changing process state, update structured data and the dashboard surface.
why: The phone view should match the machine.

name: No secret values in UI
check: Show names, consumers, source, and timestamps only.
why: Secrets stay encrypted on sbl0 and injected at process launch.

name: Dry-run before destructive action
check: Cleanup scripts must default to dry-run and require explicit apply/delete.
why: Backups and migrations are expensive to reconstruct.

name: No blind deletes
check: Delete only after classification or explicit user approval.
why: Organization must not become data loss.

name: No sideways scroll
check: Top-level and panel layouts must fit the phone width.
why: Phone is the review surface.

name: Compact grouped rows
check: Group long lists by scope and collapse repeated labels.
why: Improves scan speed and reduces scrolling.

name: Tap for details
check: Launcher stays small; details live in focused overlays.
why: Keeps the home screen stable and simple.

name: Theme contrast
check: Primary, secondary, and tertiary text tokens must pass contrast checks in light and dark themes.
why: Visual polish cannot weaken operational readability.

name: Readable facts
check: Do not use decorative gray or ultra-light weight for project facts, paths, TODOs, or state labels.
why: Dense phone tiles need fast scanning.

name: Clamp not shrink
check: Clamp long text and reveal detail on tap instead of shrinking fonts below readable sizes.
why: Information hierarchy beats tiny text.

name: Build before restart
check: Run lint and build before restarting a dashboard service.
why: Catches TypeScript and static export errors before deploy.

name: Verify endpoint
check: Check HTTP 200 after service restart.
why: Confirms phone-facing LAN deployment is actually serving.

name: Source/generated boundary
check: Classify source, generated, cache, runtime, and archive before moving.
why: Keeps rebuilds possible and project roots clean.

name: Promote repeated commands
check: If a command is useful twice, add it to Shortcuts.
why: Builds operational memory without Markdown.

name: Concrete final state
check: Report what changed, what was verified, and what remains risky.
why: Keeps collaboration actionable.
